overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: FA's post-term covenant not to compete (2yr/20mi) may not be enforceable under California law
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: Post-term re-entry into California is meaningfully more open than the FA suggests; CA prospective franchisees should not assume the 2yr/20mi block is enforceable

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Utah arbitration / Utah venue may not be enforceable; CA Bus. & Prof. Code §20040.5 and Federal Arbitration Act may control
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate or arbitrate in California rather than Utah

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Utah law selection 'may not be enforceable under California law'
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: California Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act may apply notwithstanding the choice-of-law clause

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: CA Bus. & Prof. Code §§20000–20043 provide rights to franchisee on termination, transfer, nonrenewal; if FA conflicts with the law, the law controls
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: CA franchisees get broader procedural protections than the FA discloses

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy is not necessarily an automatic franchise-killer in CA

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Litigation outside Minnesota cannot be required; jury trial waiver cannot be required; liquidated damages, termination penalties, judgment notes not enforceable
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain jury trial, in-state venue, and full damages remedies notwithstanding the FA's Utah-only and LD provisions

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: 90-day notice of termination with 60-day cure; 180-day notice for non-renewal
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: MN franchisees get materially longer cure and non-renewal notice periods than the FA's 3/10/30/90-day curable-default ladder

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Franchisor cannot require franchisee to assent to a general release (MN Rules 2860.4400(D))
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: MN transferring franchisees do not have to sign the general release the FA requires

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Consent to transfer cannot be unreasonably withheld (MN Stat 80C.14)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: Reinforces but extends the FA's 'not unreasonably withheld' standard with statutory enforcement

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: No acknowledgement waives state franchise law claims including fraud-in-the-inducement; supersedes any contrary FA term
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain fraud claims regardless of FA acknowledgements

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: NSF fees capped at $30 per MN Stat §604.113
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: Caps the FA's late-fee/NSF mechanics in MN

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: NY-mandated cover-page language about not negotiating less-favorable terms; expanded Item 3 disclosure attestations re felony, antitrust, securities, fraud
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: NY franchisees receive additional fraud/antitrust/securities representations on top of the federal Item 3

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in conflict of laws
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WFIPA controls notwithstanding Utah choice of law

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site = Washington (or mutually agreed at the time, or as determined by the arbitrator); WA franchisees may bring suit in WA on WFIPA claims
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA franchisees do NOT have to travel to Salt Lake County for arbitration

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: General release / waiver in FA is VOID except where executed pursuant to a negotiated post-effectiveness settlement with independent counsel (RCW 19.100.220(2)); release on renewal/transfer also VOID
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA transferring or renewing franchisees do not have to sign general releases. This is a stronger protection than the typical state addendum.

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Provisions limiting WFIPA statute of limitations or jury trial rights may not be enforceable
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA franchisees retain jury trial and full statutory limitations period

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Transfer fees collectable only to extent they reflect franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs in effecting transfer
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA $10,000 flat transfer fee may be capped to actual cost — typically much less

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate FA under any grounds permitted under state law (broader than FA §15A which limits termination to franchisor breach)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA franchisees have broader termination rights than the FA discloses

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: Buy-back clauses (franchisor purchase during term) limited
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: WA constrains franchisor option to purchase mid-term

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: (Pages 173–175 — IL FDD/FA/ADA addenda enumerated; clause text not extracted in this run)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: IL Franchise Disclosure Act typically voids out-of-state forum and law selection

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: (Pages 176–178 — MD FDD/FA/ADA addenda enumerated; clause text not extracted in this run)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: MD typically voids general releases and out-of-state venue/law

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: (Page 185 — ND addendum enumerated; clause text not extracted in this run)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: ND typically voids out-of-state venue, law, jury waiver, and limits non-compete enforceability

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: (Page 186 — RI addendum enumerated; clause text not extracted in this run)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: RI typically voids out-of-state forum and law clauses

overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.override_summary: (Page 187 — VA addendum enumerated; clause text not extracted in this run)
overrides_extracted_from_pages_read.why_it_matters: VA Retail Franchising Act provides good-cause termination protection

incomplete_per_state_extraction_note: IL/MD/ND/RI/VA addenda were located by page but not clause-extracted in this test run. A non-test run should pull each addendum's full text into structured form for the canonical state_addenda_overrides family.
